model_comparison: The comparison between the two models reveals a significant difference in quality. Model 1 perfectly executed the user's command. It read the text accurately and applied the requested emphasis on "关键" (0.0s - 0.8s) clearly and naturally. In contrast, Model 2 failed on a fundamental level. It misinterpreted the prompt and included the instructional word "说" in its output, resulting in the incorrect phrase "关键说" at the beginning. This is a critical content accuracy error. Furthermore, even in the correct portion of the text, Model 2's emphasis on "关键" (at 1.2s) was extremely subtle and ineffective, failing the core paralinguistic task. Therefore, Model 1 is significantly superior as it followed all instructions correctly while Model 2 failed on both content and feature control.
